summary: A React/TypeScript fan tool for Project Moon (Lobotomy Corporation → Library of Ruina → Limbus Company) with two main features: an interactive lore graph connecting Sinners to their literary origins across all three games, and a lore-aware team builder. Both tools share one underlying data model.

milestones.name: Shared data layer
milestones.description: Define the TypeScript schema and manually author 12–15 Sinner entries covering the clearest literary connections. This is the foundation everything else reads from.
deliverables: TypeScript types file (Sinner, Identity, EGO, LiterarySource, Theme)
JSON seed data: 12–15 Sinners with literary sources and themes
Cross-game appearance flags (lobotomy, ruina, limbus)
milestones.notes: The data work requires fan knowledge, not coding skill. Do this before any UI. Even publishing the schema as open source would be a meaningful community contribution.

milestones.name: Lore graph MVP
milestones.description: Minimal D3 force-directed graph in React rendering the 12–15 seed Sinners as nodes, with edges representing literary connections, shared themes, and cross-game continuity.
deliverables: React + D3 force graph component
Node click → detail panel (literary source, themes, game appearances)
Edge types: literary origin, thematic link, cross-game continuity
Deployed to Vercel (public URL)
milestones.notes: This is the proof-of-concept milestone. Once deployed, share with r/LimbusCompany or the wiki Discord to get early feedback and community attention.

milestones.name: Lore graph full content
milestones.description: Expand the graph with richer data: all Cantos annotated, cross-game entity connections (Wings, Abnormalities, recurring characters), spoiler-gating toggle.
deliverables: All 13 Sinners fully authored
Canto-by-canto annotation layer (spoiler-gated)
Cross-game entities: Angela/Ayin, Wings, key Abnormalities
Literary source explorer panel (book title, author, themes adapted)
Filter by: game, theme, literary origin
milestones.notes: This is the long-tail content work. Can be done incrementally — ship M2 first, then add data patch by patch.

milestones.name: Sinner profile cards
milestones.description: Rich per-Sinner pages showing: base lore summary, literary source, all available Identities and EGOs, cross-game timeline. Cross-linked from graph nodes.
deliverables: Sinner detail page / panel
Identity and EGO list per Sinner
Link out to existing wikis (limbuscompany.wiki.gg) for mechanical detail
milestones.notes: Don't compete with Prydwen on mechanical data — link out. Focus on the lore layer that they don't have.

milestones.name: Team builder (lore-aware)
milestones.description: Team composition tool that filters Identities and EGOs by literary origin, thematic cluster, and Wing affiliation — not just damage type. The unique angle vs Prydwen/GLL.
deliverables: Team slot UI (6 Sinners, 1 Identity + 1 EGO each)
Filter panel: damage type, literary origin, theme, game
Resonance highlight: pairs sharing literary source or thematic arc
Save/share team via URL params
milestones.notes: Build this after M3. By then you may have community users who can tell you exactly what filters they want.

milestones.name: Cross-game entity search
milestones.description: Single search box that spans all three games. Search 'Angela' → see her Lobotomy origin, Library arc, and Limbus appearances all in one result.
deliverables: Fuse.js fuzzy search over unified entity data
Result cards grouped by game
Deep link into graph nodes or profile pages

tech_stack.styling: TBD — Mantine (familiar from work) or Tailwind
tech_stack.data: Static JSON files in repo (no backend needed for MVP)

portfolio_notes.why_it_stands_out: No existing tool maps literary sources → game adaptation for Project Moon. This is the gap only a fan-developer fills.
portfolio_notes.cv_framing: Built a full-stack fan tool with D3 force graph, TypeScript data modeling, and React UI for a 70k+ Discord community.
portfolio_notes.repo_visibility: Public GitHub repo. Pin it. Write an English README explaining the project and the literary connection layer.

next_action: Ask Claude to write the TypeScript data model for the shared data layer (M1).

key_decisions: Start with data layer before any UI
Lore graph first — more unique than team builder
Team builder added later as second tab once community attention exists
Do not compete with Prydwen/GLL on mechanical data — link out, focus on lore layer
Static JSON data is fine for MVP, no backend needed
